description_long: This is a handheld digital Geiger counter, specifically the GCA-07 model, designed for detecting and measuring radiation levels. The device features a rectangular body with a digital display and several physical buttons and switches on the front panel, along with indicator lights and a radiation warning symbol. Attached to the side is a cylindrical Geiger-Müller tube, held in place by a bracket and connected via a cable that loops to the top of the device. The casing appears to be made of light-colored plastic or metal, with a matte finish and some printed labels and branding. The back of the device is plain except for a label, while the sides show the protruding switches and the cylindrical sensor. This instrument is typically used by professionals or hobbyists for environmental monitoring, safety checks, or educational purposes in urban or industrial settings.
description: A handheld digital Geiger counter with a rectangular body, digital display, and attached cylindrical sensor for radiation detection. The device features physical buttons, indicator lights, and a cable connecting the sensor to the main unit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, control buttons, indicator lights, and the attached cylindrical Geiger-Müller tube.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile, protruding switches, and the cable connection at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back panel with a label and the full length of the attached sensor and cable.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the cylindrical sensor and the cable running along the side of the device.
